name: DeepTeam
tagline: Red team your LLM apps with simulated jailbreaks, prompt injection, and 50+ vulnerability checks
seoTitle: DeepTeam: Open-Source LLM Red Teaming Framework
metaDescription: DeepTeam is an open-source LLM red teaming framework. Simulate jailbreaks and prompt injection to find bias, PII leakage, and SQL injection in your AI apps.
overview: DeepTeam is an open-source red teaming framework for LLM systems, built by the team behind DeepEval. It works like penetration testing for AI apps: it simulates adversarial attacks such as jailbreaking, prompt injection, and multi-turn exploitation to uncover vulnerabilities including bias, PII leakage, and SQL injection in your agents, RAG pipelines, and chatbots.

It is aimed at developers and security engineers who need to test the safety, security, and compliance of LLM-powered applications before they reach production. You point DeepTeam at your model through a callback function, choose which vulnerabilities and attacks to run, and it returns a risk assessment.

As an evaluation and testing framework, DeepTeam runs locally on your machine and uses LLM-as-a-judge metrics to produce binary pass/fail scores with reasoning. You can use any LLM you choose as the judge, and pair it with the optional Confident AI platform to track results over time.
features: 50+ ready-to-use vulnerabilities across data privacy, responsible AI, security, safety, and business categories, each scored with LLM-as-a-judge metrics that run locally
Simulated attacks including jailbreaking, prompt injection, and multi-turn exploitation to probe your system the way a real attacker would
Detects concrete risks such as PII leakage, prompt leakage, bias, toxicity, SQL injection, shell injection, SSRF, and broken access control (BFLA/BOLA/RBAC)
Works against AI agents, RAG pipelines, and chatbots through a simple model callback you define
Use any LLM of your choice as the judge model; everything runs locally on your machine
Optional guardrails to block these issues in production, plus integration with the Confident AI platform for managing and sharing results

gettingStarted.intro: Install DeepTeam with pip, then wrap your LLM app in a callback and run a red team scan against the vulnerabilities and attacks you care about.

steps.heading: Install DeepTeam
steps.body: Install the package from PyPI. DeepTeam runs locally and is built on DeepEval.

steps.heading: Run a red team scan
steps.body: Define a model callback that calls your LLM app, pick the vulnerabilities and attacks to test, and run red_team to get a risk assessment.
steps.code:
from deepteam import red_team
from deepteam.vulnerabilities import Bias
from deepteam.attacks.single_turn import PromptInjection

async def model_callback(input: str) -> str:
    return f"I'm sorry but I can't answer this: {input}"

risk_assessment = red_team(
    model_callback=model_callback,
    vulnerabilities=[Bias(types=["race"])],
    attacks=[PromptInjection()]
)

useCases: Run a pre-release security pass on a customer-facing chatbot to check for PII and prompt leakage
Test an AI agent or tool-using system for access-control flaws like BFLA, BOLA, and RBAC bypass
Probe a RAG pipeline for bias, toxicity, and injection vulnerabilities before going to production
Add red teaming to CI so regressions in safety and security are caught on every change
